useWhen: You need ACID transactions, joins, constraints and standard relational engines.
avoidWhen: The workload needs extreme horizontal/global scale or highly flexible schema.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Strong ACID transactions; engine-specific isolation and replication.
primaryAccessPattern: SQL queries, joins, indexed transactions and reporting.
scalePattern: Vertical scale, read replicas and managed HA; horizontal scale depends on engine.
awsServices: Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, SQL Server, Oracle and Db2.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure SQL Database; Azure SQL Managed Instance; Azure Database for PostgreSQL; Azure Database for MySQL.
googleCloudServices: Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL, MySQL and SQL Server.
vectorAiCapability: pgvector and engine extensions where supported; connect to AI platforms.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Private networking, encryption, backups, audit logs, row/column security and database identity.
portabilityLockIn: Medium; engine compatibility improves portability.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Start with managed relational SQL unless access patterns clearly require a purpose-built NoSQL or distributed database.
importantLifecycleNote: Engine features, versions and HA models differ by provider.

useWhen: You want managed relational semantics with cloud-native replication, storage and scaling.
avoidWhen: You need exact portability to a self-managed engine or simple low-scale workloads.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: ACID with provider-specific distributed storage and HA architecture.
primaryAccessPattern: Transactional SQL with high availability and read scaling.
scalePattern: Storage/compute separation, replicas and provider-specific scaling.
awsServices: Amazon Aurora MySQL-Compatible and Aurora PostgreSQL-Compatible.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure SQL Database and Hyperscale; Azure Database for PostgreSQL for open-source workloads.
googleCloudServices: AlloyDB for PostgreSQL; Spanner for distributed/global requirements.
vectorAiCapability: Aurora/AlloyDB/PostgreSQL vector extensions; Azure SQL/PostgreSQL vector capabilities.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Provider-managed HA, encryption, backups, auditing and private endpoints.
portabilityLockIn: Medium–High; cloud-native features increase lock-in.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Choose when availability, performance or operational simplicity justify deeper provider coupling.
importantLifecycleNote: No single exact feature-equivalent trio; benchmark the actual workload.

useWhen: You want PostgreSQL ecosystem, extensions and relational transactions.
avoidWhen: You rely on unsupported extensions or require full superuser/OS control.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Strong ACID transactions using PostgreSQL semantics.
primaryAccessPattern: OLTP, JSONB, geospatial, vector and mixed application queries.
scalePattern: Single primary with replicas, sharding/scale-out options depending on service.
awsServices: Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL; Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL-Compatible.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Database for PostgreSQL.
googleCloudServices: Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL; AlloyDB for PostgreSQL.
vectorAiCapability: pgvector/AI integrations; AlloyDB AI and provider embedding integrations.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Extension allowlist, network isolation, encryption, audit and privileged-access design.
portabilityLockIn: Low–Medium; PostgreSQL compatibility supports migration, but managed features differ.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Use PostgreSQL as the default relational engine when open ecosystem and portability matter.
importantLifecycleNote: Extension, replication, maintenance and performance behavior differ.

useWhen: You need a common open-source engine for web and transactional applications.
avoidWhen: You need advanced distributed SQL semantics or PostgreSQL-specific extensions.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: ACID transactions with MySQL engine behavior.
primaryAccessPattern: OLTP, indexed reads/writes and web application data.
scalePattern: Managed HA, replicas and cloud-native variants.
awsServices: Amazon RDS for MySQL; Amazon Aurora MySQL-Compatible.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Database for MySQL.
googleCloudServices: Cloud SQL for MySQL.
vectorAiCapability: Application-level embeddings/vector integration; fewer native AI extensions than PostgreSQL.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Backups, encryption, audit, private network and version compatibility.
portabilityLockIn: Low–Medium with standard MySQL; higher for Aurora-specific features.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Choose for MySQL-compatible workloads; do not migrate engines without measurable business value.
importantLifecycleNote: Cloud-native variants and supported versions still differ.

useWhen: You need Microsoft SQL Server compatibility, T-SQL, enterprise tooling or lift-and-shift.
avoidWhen: You want open-source portability or do not need SQL Server-specific features/licensing.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Strong ACID and SQL Server transaction semantics.
primaryAccessPattern: T-SQL, stored procedures, enterprise applications and reporting.
scalePattern: Managed database, managed instance or VM depending on compatibility.
awsServices: Amazon RDS for SQL Server; SQL Server on Amazon EC2.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure SQL Database; Azure SQL Managed Instance; SQL Server on Azure VMs; SQL Database in Microsoft Fabric.
googleCloudServices: Cloud SQL for SQL Server; SQL Server on Compute Engine.
vectorAiCapability: Azure has the deepest Microsoft data/AI/Fabric integration.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Licence rights, TDE, Entra/AD integration, auditing, backup and HA.
portabilityLockIn: High engine portability but licensing and Microsoft integration influence platform choice.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Azure is usually first evaluation for Microsoft estates; use RDS/Cloud SQL when strategic-cloud alignment outweighs native Microsoft integration.
importantLifecycleNote: Azure offers the broadest SQL Server operating models.

useWhen: You need Oracle compatibility for packaged or legacy enterprise systems.
avoidWhen: You can modernize to an open engine without unacceptable application risk.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Oracle ACID semantics and enterprise features.
primaryAccessPattern: Oracle SQL/PLSQL, packaged applications and enterprise transactions.
scalePattern: Managed service or dedicated Oracle cloud infrastructure integrated with hyperscaler.
awsServices: Amazon RDS for Oracle; Oracle Database@AWS; Oracle on EC2.
microsoftAzureServices: Oracle Database@Azure; Oracle on Azure VMs.
googleCloudServices: Oracle Database@Google Cloud; Oracle on Compute Engine.
vectorAiCapability: Oracle AI/vector features depend on Oracle offering; integrate with cloud AI services.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Licensing, support boundaries, network, encryption, backup and vendor responsibility.
portabilityLockIn: High commercial and technical lock-in.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Prefer replatform only when compatibility and business risk justify Oracle; distinguish hyperscaler hosting from Oracle-managed database service.
importantLifecycleNote: Commercial availability and region coverage require validation.

useWhen: You need Db2 compatibility for existing enterprise workloads.
avoidWhen: You can modernize to another engine without business risk.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Db2 transactional semantics.
primaryAccessPattern: Db2 SQL and enterprise application access.
scalePattern: Managed AWS option or partner/self-managed deployments elsewhere.
awsServices: Amazon RDS for Db2; Db2 on Amazon EC2.
microsoftAzureServices: IBM Db2 on Azure or Azure VMs.
googleCloudServices: IBM Db2 on Google Cloud / Compute Engine.
vectorAiCapability: External AI integration.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Licensing, supported editions, migration tools, backup and vendor support.
portabilityLockIn: High engine/vendor lock-in.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Use where Db2 compatibility is mandatory; otherwise assess modernization economics.
importantLifecycleNote: AWS has a direct managed RDS option; Azure/GCP are generally IBM/VM-led.

useWhen: You need strong consistency, horizontal scale and multi-region resilience for mission-critical transactions.
avoidWhen: A regional managed relational database meets requirements; distributed SQL adds cost and design complexity.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Strong transactions across distributed data; exact semantics vary.
primaryAccessPattern: Global OLTP, high availability, relational queries and cross-region access.
awsServices: Amazon Aurora DSQL.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure SQL Database Hyperscale and distributed application patterns; no identical global distributed SQL equivalent.
googleCloudServices: Spanner.
vectorAiCapability: Spanner supports relational, graph, key-value and search features; AI integration and vector capabilities.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Region topology, consistency, quorum behavior, data residency and failover testing.
portabilityLockIn: High provider-specific lock-in.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Only select distributed SQL after proving regional relational databases cannot meet scale, availability or geography requirements.
importantLifecycleNote: Spanner is the most established direct global-distributed SQL service; alternatives differ materially.

useWhen: Workload demand is variable and you want automated capacity management.
avoidWhen: You need fixed predictable latency, sustained high utilization or engine features not available in serverless tiers.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Standard relational transactions; capacity behavior is service-specific.
primaryAccessPattern: Intermittent OLTP, development, SaaS tenants and variable workloads.
scalePattern: Pause/resume or fine-grained scaling depending on service.
awsServices: Aurora Serverless v2; RDS Proxy for connection management.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure SQL Database serverless.
googleCloudServices: Spanner autoscaling or AlloyDB/Cloud SQL managed scaling patterns; no exact Cloud SQL serverless equivalent.
vectorAiCapability: Same engine vector/AI options as underlying service.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Cold starts, min/max capacity, connection limits, cost floors and quota.
portabilityLockIn: Medium–High due service-specific scaling model.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Use when measured utilization variability creates real savings without violating latency objectives.
importantLifecycleNote: Serverless behavior is not directly equivalent.

useWhen: You need predictable low-latency access by primary key at very large scale.
avoidWhen: You need joins, complex ad-hoc queries or multi-record relational constraints.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Atomic item operations; transaction and consistency options vary.
primaryAccessPattern: Get/put by key, counters, sessions, profiles and high-scale state.
awsServices: Amazon DynamoDB.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL or Table; Azure Table Storage for simpler requirements.
googleCloudServices: Bigtable for wide-column/key access; Firestore for application documents.
vectorAiCapability: DynamoDB/OpenSearch integrations; Cosmos/Firestore vector options differ.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Partition-key design, hot keys, consistency, encryption, backup and fine-grained access.
portabilityLockIn: High provider lock-in because APIs and data modelling differ.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Choose only when key-based access dominates; model access patterns before selecting the service.
importantLifecycleNote: Bigtable and Firestore serve different subpatterns rather than one exact equivalent.

useWhen: You need flexible JSON/BSON-style documents and application-centric aggregates.
avoidWhen: You need complex joins, strict relational constraints or analytics as the primary workload.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Document-level atomicity; multi-document transactions vary.
primaryAccessPattern: Document lookup, nested data, flexible schema and application APIs.
scalePattern: Horizontal partitioning with indexes and replicas.
awsServices: Amazon DocumentDB; DynamoDB document structures.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL; Azure DocumentDB.
googleCloudServices: Firestore in Native mode; Firestore with MongoDB compatibility.
vectorAiCapability: Native or integrated vector search on Azure DocumentDB/Cosmos; Firestore and external vector services on GCP.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Schema governance, index cost, partition keys, document size, consistency and backup.
portabilityLockIn: Medium–High; compatibility level determines portability.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Choose document storage when aggregates are retrieved together and schema evolves; avoid treating it as relational SQL without joins.
importantLifecycleNote: Mongo-compatible and native document APIs are separate decisions.

useWhen: You need MongoDB drivers/tools or migration compatibility.
avoidWhen: You require unsupported MongoDB features or true MongoDB implementation guarantees.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: MongoDB-compatible document transactions and consistency vary by product.
primaryAccessPattern: BSON documents, Mongo query API and flexible indexes.
awsServices: Amazon DocumentDB with MongoDB compatibility.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure DocumentDB; Azure Cosmos DB for MongoDB.
googleCloudServices: Firestore with MongoDB compatibility.
vectorAiCapability: Azure DocumentDB includes vector search; other services can integrate vector search.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Compatibility percentage, wire protocol, feature gaps, version support and migration tests.
portabilityLockIn: Medium; compatibility can reduce app changes but does not ensure identical behavior.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Run compatibility testing with real queries, indexes and drivers before migration approval.
importantLifecycleNote: All are compatible services, not necessarily identical MongoDB implementations.

useWhen: You need huge sparse tables, predictable key/range access and very high throughput.
avoidWhen: You need joins, rich transactions or flexible secondary querying.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Row/partition-level consistency and limited transaction scope.
primaryAccessPattern: Partition key plus column/range scans; telemetry and large operational datasets.
awsServices: Amazon Keyspaces for Apache Cassandra; DynamoDB for key-value alternatives.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Managed Instance for Apache Cassandra; Cosmos DB Cassandra API; HBase on HDInsight.
googleCloudServices: Bigtable.
vectorAiCapability: Bigtable/Keyspaces can support ML feature and operational data patterns; vector search usually separate.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Partition design, compaction, consistency, replication and hotspot management.
portabilityLockIn: Medium–High; Cassandra compatibility improves portability.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Use only for access patterns that benefit from wide-column scale; operational complexity is not justified for standard application data.
importantLifecycleNote: Bigtable is not Cassandra, while AWS/Azure provide Cassandra-compatible options.

useWhen: You have Cassandra data models, CQL applications or hybrid Cassandra requirements.
avoidWhen: You are designing a new workload without a Cassandra-specific reason.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Tunable consistency and partition-oriented transactions.
primaryAccessPattern: CQL, partition scans and high-write distributed workloads.
awsServices: Amazon Keyspaces for Apache Cassandra.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Managed Instance for Apache Cassandra; Cosmos DB for Apache Cassandra.
googleCloudServices: No direct first-party Cassandra service; Bigtable or partner Cassandra.
vectorAiCapability: External vector/AI integration.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: CQL compatibility, consistency settings, migration tooling and operational ownership.
portabilityLockIn: Medium with CQL; provider implementation differences remain.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: For new workloads, compare Cassandra against DynamoDB/Cosmos/Bigtable before accepting its operational model.
importantLifecycleNote: Google Cloud requires a different first-party model or partner product.

useWhen: Relationships and multi-hop traversal are first-class query requirements.
avoidWhen: Simple parent-child relations or standard joins are sufficient.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Graph transaction and consistency model depends on engine.
primaryAccessPattern: Traversal, path, neighborhood, fraud, network and knowledge-graph queries.
scalePattern: Scale by graph partitioning, replicas and specialized execution.
awsServices: Amazon Neptune.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Cosmos DB for Apache Gremlin.
googleCloudServices: Spanner Graph.
vectorAiCapability: GraphRAG and vector-plus-graph retrieval; Spanner Graph supports combined graph/vector patterns.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Graph model, query language, traversal depth, access control, lineage and explainability.
portabilityLockIn: High because graph languages and engine behavior differ.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Choose graph only when relationship traversal is a dominant requirement, not because the data contains relationships.
importantLifecycleNote: Gremlin, Neptune engines and Spanner Graph differ in query model and integration.

useWhen: You need high-ingest timestamped measurements, retention and time-window queries.
avoidWhen: General relational or analytical storage already meets throughput and query needs.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Append-heavy data with time-based partitioning; transaction scope is limited.
primaryAccessPattern: Time windows, aggregations, downsampling, telemetry and metrics.
scalePattern: High ingest with tiering/retention and partitioned queries.
awsServices: Amazon Timestream.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Data Explorer; Eventhouse in Microsoft Fabric; PostgreSQL time-series extensions for mixed OLTP.
googleCloudServices: Bigtable or BigQuery depending operational versus analytical access.
vectorAiCapability: Anomaly detection and ML integration through cloud AI services.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Retention, downsampling, late-arriving data, schema evolution and regional replication.
portabilityLockIn: Medium–High; query languages and storage models differ.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Distinguish operational time-series queries from large-scale analytical telemetry before choosing.
importantLifecycleNote: Azure/GCP commonly use analytical engines rather than a direct Timestream equivalent.

useWhen: You need relevance ranking, linguistic analysis, filtering and near-real-time indexing.
avoidWhen: You need a system of record with strong transactions; a search index should not be the primary database.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Eventual index consistency; source database remains authoritative.
primaryAccessPattern: Full-text, fuzzy, semantic and faceted search.
awsServices: Amazon OpenSearch Service.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure AI Search; Azure Data Explorer for log/text analytics.
googleCloudServices: Vertex AI Search for enterprise/AI search; Elastic Cloud or BigQuery for other search/analytics patterns.
vectorAiCapability: Hybrid keyword/vector search and RAG integration.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Index pipeline, ACL propagation, data freshness, sensitive content and deletion propagation.
portabilityLockIn: High API/query-engine lock-in; source data remains portable.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Treat search as a derived serving index, not the authoritative transactional store.
importantLifecycleNote: The providers target overlapping but not identical search scenarios.

useWhen: You need very large-scale approximate nearest-neighbor retrieval with filtering and low latency.
avoidWhen: Vectors are a minor feature of an existing relational/document database and a separate service adds complexity.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Vector index consistency; metadata filters and update semantics vary.
primaryAccessPattern: Embedding similarity, semantic search, recommendations and RAG.
scalePattern: Sharded ANN index and managed serving.
awsServices: Amazon OpenSearch Service vector engine.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure AI Search vector search.
googleCloudServices: Agent Platform Vector Search 2.0.
vectorAiCapability: Primary function is vector retrieval; integrate with Bedrock/Foundry/Agent Platform.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Embedding version, index rebuild, metadata ACLs, deletion, evaluation and data residency.
portabilityLockIn: High service/API lock-in.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Use dedicated vector search only when scale or latency exceeds an existing database's vector capability.
importantLifecycleNote: Index algorithms, filtering and update models differ.

useWhen: You need transactions and application data with integrated embedding search.
avoidWhen: Vector workloads are extremely large or need specialized ANN serving.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Relational ACID plus vector-index behavior.
primaryAccessPattern: SQL filters/joins combined with similarity search.
awsServices: Aurora PostgreSQL/RDS PostgreSQL with pgvector.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Database for PostgreSQL pgvector; Azure SQL vector capabilities.
googleCloudServices: AlloyDB AI; Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL pgvector; Spanner vector capabilities.
vectorAiCapability: Tight integration with RAG and operational data.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Embedding lifecycle, index tuning, query plans, row-level access and transactional consistency.
portabilityLockIn: Low–Medium using PostgreSQL; higher for provider-specific AI features.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Prefer this pattern when vectors belong to transactional records and scale remains within database capability.
importantLifecycleNote: Supported indexes, dimensions and acceleration differ.

useWhen: You need flexible documents and vector search in one operational store.
avoidWhen: You need complex joins or dedicated large-scale vector serving.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Document consistency plus vector-index semantics.
primaryAccessPattern: Document filters plus semantic similarity.
scalePattern: Partitioned document database with vector index.
awsServices: Amazon DocumentDB vector search.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Cosmos DB vector search; Azure DocumentDB vector search.
googleCloudServices: Firestore/Spanner alternatives plus Agent Platform Vector Search; verify native document-vector support for the chosen mode.
vectorAiCapability: RAG and semantic application search.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Document ACLs, partitioning, vector-index cost, deletion and embedding version.
portabilityLockIn: Medium–High due API and vector-index differences.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Select when document and vector access occur together; otherwise decouple operational data and retrieval index.
importantLifecycleNote: Google Cloud design may use separate vector service or Spanner rather than Firestore alone.

useWhen: You need sub-millisecond cache, sessions, counters, pub/sub or transient state.
avoidWhen: The data must be the sole durable system of record without an appropriate persistence design.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: In-memory operations; durability and replication depend on mode.
primaryAccessPattern: Key-value, data structures, caching, session state and rate limiting.
awsServices: Amazon ElastiCache for Valkey/Redis OSS; Amazon MemoryDB.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Managed Redis.
googleCloudServices: Memorystore for Redis, Redis Cluster and Valkey.
vectorAiCapability: Can support feature serving, agent session state and semantic cache patterns.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Eviction, persistence, backup, encryption, failover and cache-stampede protection.
portabilityLockIn: Medium; Redis protocol helps portability, managed features differ.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Use a cache to improve a system of record; choose MemoryDB-style durability only when the workload truly requires it.
importantLifecycleNote: MemoryDB is durable-primary oriented; standard caches are not equivalent.

useWhen: You need a simple distributed volatile cache using the Memcached protocol.
avoidWhen: You need durability, rich data structures, replication or advanced pub/sub.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Volatile cache with no durable transaction semantics.
primaryAccessPattern: Simple get/set cache.
awsServices: Amazon ElastiCache for Memcached.
microsoftAzureServices: No direct first-party Memcached equivalent; Azure Managed Redis is the strategic alternative.
googleCloudServices: Memorystore for Memcached.
vectorAiCapability: Limited direct AI role beyond caching.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Eviction, cache invalidation, network isolation and failure behavior.
portabilityLockIn: Low protocol lock-in, but Azure migration requires Redis/application change.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Use only when Memcached compatibility or simplicity is required; Redis/Valkey is usually the richer default.
importantLifecycleNote: Azure has no direct managed Memcached equivalent.

useWhen: You need tamper-evident, append-only records with cryptographic verification.
avoidWhen: A standard audited relational database plus immutable storage meets the requirement.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Append-only/verifiable record semantics rather than general relational querying.
primaryAccessPattern: Audit records, multiparty evidence and integrity verification.
awsServices: Amazon QLDB is fully shut down; use Amazon Managed Blockchain or alternative verifiable-ledger patterns.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Confidential Ledger.
googleCloudServices: Partner ledger/blockchain services; Blockchain Node Engine is not an exact confidential-ledger equivalent.
vectorAiCapability: Can store AI decision/evidence hashes, but does not replace complete audit logs.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Key custody, verifier independence, retention, privacy and correction conflicts.
portabilityLockIn: High specialized-service lock-in.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Use only where cryptographic verification is an explicit requirement; note that immutable records can conflict with deletion obligations.
importantLifecycleNote: Amazon QLDB reached end of support on 31 July 2025.

useWhen: You need managed access to blockchain networks, nodes or chain data.
avoidWhen: The business requirement does not need decentralized or shared-ledger properties.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Blockchain consensus and chain-specific transaction model.
primaryAccessPattern: Node RPC, smart contracts, network membership and chain analytics.
awsServices: Amazon Managed Blockchain.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Confidential Ledger and partner blockchain offerings.
googleCloudServices: Blockchain Node Engine, Blockchain RPC and Blockchain Analytics.
vectorAiCapability: AI can analyze chain data; blockchain is not an AI governance substitute.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Key management, smart-contract risk, privacy, finality, network governance and regulation.
portabilityLockIn: Very high technology and ecosystem lock-in.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Require a clear multiparty trust problem before selecting blockchain.
importantLifecycleNote: Services cover different layers: ledger, nodes, RPC and analytics.

useWhen: You need large-scale analytical SQL, BI concurrency and columnar processing.
avoidWhen: You need low-latency row-level OLTP as the primary workload.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Analytical consistency; not a general OLTP transaction system.
primaryAccessPattern: Scans, aggregations, BI, ELT and historical analysis.
awsServices: Amazon Redshift and Redshift Serverless.
microsoftAzureServices: Microsoft Fabric Warehouse; Azure Synapse Analytics.
googleCloudServices: BigQuery.
vectorAiCapability: Embedded ML/AI, vector/search and integration with provider AI platforms.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Data governance, workload management, semantic layer, sharing, row/column controls and cost governance.
portabilityLockIn: High service-specific SQL/operating-model lock-in.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Select from data gravity, BI ecosystem, concurrency, governance and cost—not only benchmark speed.
importantLifecycleNote: Architecture and commercial models differ materially.

useWhen: You need high-ingest telemetry and interactive analysis over recent events.
avoidWhen: You need normalized transactional updates and complex relational constraints.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Append/ingest-optimized analytical consistency.
primaryAccessPattern: Time-window analysis, logs, telemetry and near-real-time dashboards.
scalePattern: Distributed ingestion and columnar analytical storage.
awsServices: Amazon OpenSearch Service; Timestream; Redshift streaming patterns.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Data Explorer; Eventhouse in Microsoft Fabric.
googleCloudServices: Bigtable for operational serving; BigQuery for analytical queries.
vectorAiCapability: Anomaly detection and AI-assisted analysis integrations.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Retention, ingestion reliability, schema evolution, PII in logs and query-cost controls.
portabilityLockIn: Medium–High due query-language differences.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Choose based on required ingest latency and interactive query pattern; separate hot operational and historical analytical tiers when useful.
importantLifecycleNote: No exact one-to-one trio.

databaseModelCapability: Serverless mobile and web application database
useWhen: You need client SDKs, offline sync, real-time updates and minimal backend operations.
avoidWhen: You need complex relational reporting, strict cross-record transactions or database portability.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Application-oriented transactions and consistency.
primaryAccessPattern: Mobile/web documents, sync, listeners and user-scoped data.
awsServices: AWS AppSync with DynamoDB/Amplify Data.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Cosmos DB plus app services; Microsoft Dataverse for Power Platform/Dynamics scenarios.
googleCloudServices: Firestore in Native mode / Firebase.
vectorAiCapability: Integrated app AI and vector patterns vary.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Client security rules, identity integration, offline data, tenancy and deletion.
portabilityLockIn: High SDK/data-model lock-in.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Use when developer experience and real-time client sync outweigh relational flexibility and portability.
importantLifecycleNote: Firestore is the most integrated first-party mobile backend database.

useWhen: You need business entities, workflow, security and low-code application integration.
avoidWhen: You need unrestricted SQL, high-throughput processing or database-engine control.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Platform-managed relational/business data semantics.
primaryAccessPattern: Business entities, APIs, workflow and role/row-level security.
awsServices: AWS App Studio with AWS databases selected separately.
microsoftAzureServices: Microsoft Dataverse.
googleCloudServices: AppSheet databases/connectors and Google Cloud databases selected separately.
vectorAiCapability: Copilot/agent integration with business data.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Business roles, row/column security, environment governance, data export and integration.
portabilityLockIn: Very high application-platform lock-in.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Treat the business data platform as part of the application suite, not as a general database replacement.
importantLifecycleNote: Microsoft Dataverse is the clearest first-party business data platform.

useWhen: You need the same PostgreSQL-compatible engine across cloud, on-premises or disconnected environments.
avoidWhen: A fully managed single-cloud database provides sufficient requirements and lower operational burden.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: PostgreSQL transactions; operational responsibility varies by deployment.
primaryAccessPattern: Hybrid transactional SQL and application modernization.
awsServices: PostgreSQL on EKS/EC2 or partner databases; Aurora is AWS-only managed.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Arc-enabled PostgreSQL/partner options and Azure Database for PostgreSQL in Azure.
googleCloudServices: AlloyDB Omni and AlloyDB for PostgreSQL.
vectorAiCapability: pgvector and AI integration.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Patch responsibility, backup, HA, support boundary, licence and version parity.
portabilityLockIn: Lower engine lock-in but higher operational burden.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Choose hybrid portability only when deployment constraints justify operating more of the database stack.
importantLifecycleNote: AlloyDB Omni provides a notable first-party hybrid option.

databaseModelCapability: Database migration and change data capture
useWhen: You need low-downtime migration, replication or ongoing CDC between databases.
avoidWhen: A simple offline export/import meets downtime and volume requirements.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Preserves source transaction ordering according to tool capabilities.
primaryAccessPattern: Initial load plus continuous change replication.
scalePattern: Parallel migration workers and streaming replication.
awsServices: AWS Database Migration Service and Schema Conversion Tool.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Database Migration Service and Azure Data Factory.
googleCloudServices: Database Migration Service and Datastream.
vectorAiCapability: Feeds analytics/RAG/AI data pipelines after migration.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Schema conversion, validation, cutover, rollback, encryption, credentials and reconciliation.
portabilityLockIn: Migration tooling is temporary; target database creates long-term lock-in.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Select target architecture first; the migration tool should follow source/target compatibility and downtime requirements.
importantLifecycleNote: Feature coverage differs by source/target engine.

databaseModelCapability: Database estate discovery and fleet management
useWhen: You need centralized visibility of database versions, risk, ownership and modernization opportunities.
avoidWhen: The estate is small enough for direct service-level management.
transactionAndConsistencyProfile: Administrative metadata rather than workload transactions.
primaryAccessPattern: Inventory, recommendations, vulnerability and lifecycle management.
awsServices: AWS Systems Manager, RDS fleet views, Config and Migration Hub patterns.
microsoftAzureServices: Azure Arc-enabled data services, Azure Resource Graph and database estate tools.
googleCloudServices: Database Center.
vectorAiCapability: Can identify data sources for AI/RAG and modernization.
governanceAndSecurityFocus: Ownership, unsupported versions, public exposure, backup posture, encryption and patch level.
portabilityLockIn: Low; metadata management does not lock the database workload.
solutionEngineerDecisionRule: Create database inventory before rationalization, migration or AI data-access programmes.
importantLifecycleNote: Google Database Center is the most explicit dedicated fleet experience.
